task_completion.description: The target image is opened in the image editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP interface is visible with a photograph loaded onto the main canvas.

task_completion.description: The Filters > Decor menu is accessed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Filters' dropdown menu is open, and the 'Decor' submenu is expanded or being hovered over.

task_completion.description: The Round Corners dialog box is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog window titled 'Round Corners' (Script-Fu) is visible on the screen, showing options for edge radius and adding drop-shadow/background.

task_completion.description: The rounded corners effect is applied to the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image on the canvas no longer has sharp 90-degree corners; the corners are visibly curved/rounded.

task_completion.description: The rounded corners have a transparent background.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The areas immediately outside the curved corners of the image display a checkerboard pattern, which is the standard indicator for alpha channel transparency in GIMP.

integrity.description: The agent used the GIMP graphical user interface to apply the effect.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with GIMP's menus and dialogs, rather than terminal windows running ImageMagick commands (e.g., 'convert' or 'mogrify').

integrity.description: The agent edited the provided photograph.
integrity.visual_evidence: The image on the canvas is a real photograph, not a blank canvas, solid color, or a newly synthesized image.

integrity.description: The agent used the built-in filter rather than manually erasing the corners.
integrity.visual_evidence: The rounded corners are perfectly smooth, uniform, and symmetrical, indicating a programmatic filter was used rather than manual, uneven strokes with the Eraser tool.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires applying GIMP's 'Filters > Decor > Round Corners' tool. A successful application will result in the image having curved corners with the exterior corner regions becoming transparent. In GIMP, this transparency must be visually confirmed by the presence of a checkerboard pattern in the corner areas.
